name: shoprite-vs-spar
title: RESILIENCE SCORECARD - who weathers financial uncertainty better?
subtitle: Score 1 (most fragile) to 5 (most resilient). Scored primarily for Scenario B (Strait shut) - the stress test. Analyst judgement.

rows.why_a: Net CASH R8.1bn; only ~US$7m USD debt. Can fund price cuts & store growth through a downturn.
rows.why_b: Net DEBT R5.4bn at 1.74x; de-gearing IS the equity thesis and a high-rate path stalls it.

rows.why_a: FY2025 HEPS +15.8%; H1 FY2026 +7.9% - growing into the shock.
rows.why_b: H1 FY2026 HEPS -55.5% (199.9c); already in acute distress before any oil shock.

rows.why_a: Trading margin 5.9% and expanding (+40bps); operating leverage intact.
rows.why_b: Operating margin halved to 1.1% at H1 (from 2.1%); thin and compressing.

rows.why_a: Mass-market VALUE leader (Shoprite, Usave) - the prime trade-down WINNER as shoppers downgrade.
rows.why_b: Mid-market franchise grocers LOSE share to value chains; trade-down flows away from SPAR.

rows.why_a: Owned fleet; diesel only R335m in FY2025; scale & efficiency cushion a fuel rise.
rows.why_b: Wholesale/distribution model - fuel is core cost-to-serve; KZN DC already wounded; flagged as H2 headwind.

rows.why_a: Largely cash-based retail; limited customer credit exposure.
rows.why_b: Finances independent retailers; debtor impairments already rising - a retailer-solvency cycle would bite.

rows.why_a: ~85% RSA, minimal FX - little translation upside in a weak-rand world.
rows.why_b: Ireland (BWG, EUR1.74bn) is a genuine rand hedge: weak rand flatters euro earnings (~270bps). SPP's one edge.

rows.why_a: Stable, proven team; consistent share-gain track record.
rows.why_b: Entire C-suite new since 1 Mar 2026; KZN + SAP execution risk; 61% pay revolt.

rows.why_a: 781c (+9.7%) at 1.75x cover; sustainable through the cycle.
rows.why_b: Nil for two straight years; no timeline; FY2026 setback pushes resumption further out.

rows.why_a: Premium ~20x / 2.79% yield - quality, but vulnerable to de-rating if growth stalls.
rows.why_b: Cheap (~R60-65, -30% in 90d) but a value-trap risk until the turnaround proves out.

avg_note_a: Shoprite scores higher on every structural axis except FX.
avg_note_b: SPAR's only relative edge is the euro (Ireland) rand hedge.
verdict: Yes - Shoprite is clearly better positioned than The SPAR Group to weather financial uncertainty, in BOTH scenarios. In Scenario A (Strait open) both improve: Shoprite is a steady compounder that may modestly LAG a cyclical relief rally, while SPAR - geared and distressed - has the higher upside torque IF its self-help (KZN, SAP, cost, retailer loyalty) delivers; that is a higher-risk recovery bet, not resilience. In Scenario B (Strait shut) the gap is widest: Shoprite is a defensive OUTPERFORMER (non-discretionary food, value/trade-down leader, net cash funds a price war), whereas the same oil shock stacks rising rates, a stalled de-gearing, a higher legacy-debt drag, fuel pressure on a distribution-heavy cost base, trade-down OUT of its independents and rising retailer/debtor risk on top of an already-halved margin and a suspended dividend. Own Shoprite for resilience; SPAR is a leveraged recovery option that needs BOTH the macro (A) and execution to go right.
